title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In 1782, Captain Roger Umpleby built a house on a headland on a small island off the coast of Loch Cardip, designed to withstand harsh sea conditions. Following his death, the property passed to distant relatives. By 1922, Arthur Umpleby renovated the Georgian-style house and transformed the island into the Captain's Flag Hotel by adding a concrete causeway, tennis courts, a terrace, and a private bay with a pontoon. The hotel underwent further expansion in 1934, adding a cocktail bar and dining room.

Hercule Poirot is currently a guest at the hotel, relaxing on the beach in a white suit and Panama hat. While lounging on the terrace, he is subjected to the monotonous rambling of Mrs. Gardner, who is knitting while her husband, Mr. Odel Gardner, rests nearby. Mrs. Gardner discusses how Mr. Kyles of Cook's Travel Agency recommended the hotel for its scenery and sanitation. She expresses her admiration for Miss Dunn of Rossmond's and reveals she knows of Poirot's reputation through Miss Robson, who spoke of his work on the Linnet Ridgeway murder case in Egypt.

Other guests present include the blunt Miss Bustard, Colonel Barron, who is watching swimsuit models, and Major Barry. When Mrs. Gardner suggests Poirot might be there for professional reasons, Poirot insists he is only there to relax. The conversation shifts to the people sunbathing on the beach, whom Poirot jokingly compares to bodies.
